# Telemetry selectors report (1d)
# args: {__name__=~"e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum|e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum|e2etelemetry:max_allone:max|e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge"}
# Results depend on the queried Prometheus instance and may differ elsewhere.
selectors:
  - selector: '{__name__=~"e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum|e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum|e2etelemetry:max_allone:max|e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge"}'
    metrics:
      - name: e2etelemetry:max_allone:max
        peak_series: N
        value_range: V
        recording_rule:
          file: RULEFILE
          expr: max(e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge)
          tree: |-
            └── e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge
                  <expr: label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "a", "", "")
                    or
                    label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "b", "", "")>
                  <value_range: V> <peak_series: N> <file: RULEFILE>
      - name: e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum
        peak_series: N
        value_range: V
        recording_rule:
          file: RULEFILE
          expr: sum(e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge)
          tree: |-
            └── e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge
                  <expr: label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "a", "", "")
                    or
                    label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "b", "", "")>
                  <value_range: V> <peak_series: N> <file: RULEFILE>
      - name: e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge
        peak_series: N
        value_range: V
        recording_rule:
          file: RULEFILE
          expr: e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge and on () (sum(e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge) == 2)
          tree: |-
            ├── e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge
            │     <expr: label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "a", "", "")
            │       or
            │       label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "b", "", "")>
            │     <value_range: V> <peak_series: N> <file: RULEFILE>
            └── e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge
                  <expr: label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "a", "", "")
                    or
                    label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "b", "", "")>
                  <value_range: V> <peak_series: N> <file: RULEFILE>
        labels:
          - name: instance
            distinct_values: 2
            sample_values: [a, b]
      - name: e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum
        peak_series: N
        value_range: V
        recording_rule:
          file: RULEFILE
          expr: sum(e2etelemetry:mixed_bool:gauge)
          tree: |-
            └── e2etelemetry:mixed_bool:gauge
                  <expr: label_replace(vector(1), "instance", "a", "", "")
                    or
                    label_replace(vector(0), "instance", "b", "", "")>
                  <value_range: V> <peak_series: N> <file: RULEFILE>
    failing_checks:
      count: 9
      messages:
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:max_allone:max" is defined in CMO, it should probably live in the owning component operator
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum" is defined in CMO, it should probably live in the owning component operator
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge" is defined in CMO, it should probably live in the owning component operator
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum" is defined in CMO, it should probably live in the owning component operator
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum" uses sum() on "e2etelemetry:allone_bool:gauge" which currently only has values 0 and 1, if this is a boolean/presence metric, max(), min(), or group() may be more appropriate
        - recording rule "e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum" uses sum() on "e2etelemetry:mixed_bool:gauge" which currently only has values 0 and 1, if this is a boolean/presence metric, max(), min(), or group() may be more appropriate
        - matcher __name__=~"e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum|e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum|e2etelemetry:max_allone:max|e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge" alternation is unsorted, consider using __name__=~"e2etelemetry:max_allone:max|e2etelemetry:sum_allone:sum|e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge|e2etelemetry:sum_mixed:sum"
        - metric "e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge" label "instance" has no matcher, consider adding one to bound cardinality, e.g. instance=~"val1|val2"
        - metric "e2etelemetry:sum_bool_eq:gauge" label "instance" may be sensitive (PII risk), consider aggregating it away in the recording rule
